original: Bluey’s World officially opens in Brisbane, Australia, an immersive experience based on the animated children's TV show "Bluey"
Tacoma Narrows (Galloping Gertie) Bridge collapses in Washington
Chinese taikonaut Wang Yaping is the first Chinese female spacewalker, completing a 6 hour space walk as part of the Shenzhou 13 mission
Great Lakes Storm the "White Hurricane" begins with 90 mph winds and 35 foot waves, (lasts till 10 Nov), will sink 19 ships and strand 19 more, killing over 250 people
Cargo ship Mary Celeste sails from Staten Island for Genoa, mysteriously found abandoned four weeks later
Magic Johnsonannounces he has HIV and retires from the LA Lakers
World's oldest figurative painting of a beast at least 40,000 years old identified in Lubang Jeriji Saléh cave, Indonesian Borneo
Ensisheim Meteorite strikes a wheat field near the village of Ensisheim in Alsace, France. Oldest meteorite with a known date of impact.
Humans 1st walked upright 12 million years ago (not 6 million as previously thought), according to study a new species of ape found in Bavaria, Germany, published in "Nature"
New Jersey becomes the first state to allow girls into Little League
Lise Meitner, Austrian-Swedish physicist (discovered the nuclear fission of uranium), born in Vienna, Austria (d. 1968)
Alexa Canady, 1st African-American female neurosurgeon and educator, born in Lansing, Michigan
Kamala Harrismakes US history as the 1st woman and 1st woman of color to be elected to the vice presidency (date election called)
Eleven German miners are rescued from a collapsed mine after 14 days in the Wunder von Lengede (Miracle of Lengede)
Robert Goddarddemonstrates tube-launched solid propellant rockets
Lewis and ClarkExpedition first sights the Pacific Ocean at the mouth of the Columbia River
"Bean" film directed by Mel Smith and starringRowan Atkinsonas Mr. Bean opens in the US
Surveyor 6 launched for soft landing on Moon
Edward Bouchet is the first African American to receive a Ph.D. from a US college (Yale)
First air freight shipment is undertaken by the Wright Brothers and department store owner Max Morehouse from Dayton, Ohio, to Columbus, Ohio

Australian cricket all-rounder Glenn Maxwell hits 201 not out to guide his team to a 3 wicket World Cup win over Afghanistan in Mumbai; first to score double-ton in a ODI run chase; first non-opener to 200
Pierre Gassendi observes 1st ever transit of Mercury predicted by Kepler
Danica Roem becomes the first US transgender person to be elected to a state legislature in the US in North Virginia
Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) opens in the Hecksher Building in New York - its first exhibition featuresCezanne, Gauguin,Seuratandvan Gogh
Konrad Lorenz, Austrian zoologist and ethologist (Man & His Ideas, Nobel Prize 1973), born in Vienna, Austria (d. 1989)
First broadcast of "Buck Rogers in the 25th Century" on CBS Radio
Mary Robinson is elected the first female president of Ireland
Jeannette Rankin(Rep-R-Montana) is elected to Congress as its first woman Representative
Sierra Leone is declared free of Ebola by the World Health Organization (death toll 4,000)
Queen Elizabeth IIconfirms she is no longer buying clothes made with real fur
Hongjoong [Kim Hong Joong], South Korean captain of K-pop band ATEEZ, born in Anyang, South Korea
Bethany Mota, American video blogger, born in Merced County, California
Richard G Hatcher elected 1st African-American mayor of Gary, Indiana, served 1968-88
Carl Stokes elected first African American mayor of a major US city - Cleveland, Ohio
New York Yankee catcherElston Howardis first African-American to be voted AL MVP
Arthur L. Mitchell, becomes first African American Democratic US congressman (Illinois)
STS 51-A launch scrubbed because of high shear winds
Canadian Pacific Railway completed at Craigellachie - connects British Columbia with Eastern Canada
Battle of Tippecanoe: GeneralWilliam Henry Harrisondefeats the Native Americans of the Tecumesh Confederation
Extreme smog in Delhi, India, leads Indian Medical Association to declare "a state of medical emergency"
US State Colorado accepts female suffrage
Supreme Court of Baltimore bans segregation in public recreational areas
Verney Cameron is the first European to cross equatorial Africa
Lunar Orbiter 2 launched by US
Edwin Hall, American physicist (discovered Hall effect), born in Gorham, Maine (d. 1938)
Emil du Bois-Reymond, German physiologist, co-discoverer of nerve action potential and founder of modern electrophysiology, born in Berlin, Germany (d. 1896)
William Stukeley, English physician and archaeologist (pioneered investigation of Stonehenge and Avebury), born in Holbeach, Lincolnshire (d. 1765)
Jean-Baptiste Eugène Estienne, French general (Father of The Tank), born in Condé en Barrois, France (d. 1936)
St Petersburg floods to 4.20m, city's greatest flood
1st US President to broadcast in a foreign language -FDR, in French
NYC elects it's 1st African American mayor (David Dinkins) & 1st female comptroller (Elizabeth Holtzman)
United Press erroneously reports WWI armistice had been signed
First named reference to royal trumpeter John Blanke, the first black person whose image survives from Tudor England, in payment for services byHenry VII
Meharry Medical College forms at Central Tennessee College
Kidnapped AKZO director Herrema freed in Ireland
Pennsylvania voters overturn blue law, by permitting Sunday sports
The oldest performing musical organization in the United States is founded in Stoughton, Massachusetts, as the Stoughton Musical Society
Gervasio Deferr, Spanish gymnast (Olympic gold 2000, 2004), born in Premià de Mar, Barcelona
Mark Philippoussis, Australian tennis star (140 MPH serve), born in Melbourne, Australia
Yunjin Kim, South Korean-American actress (Sun-Lost), born in Seoul, South Korea
